content.full_text:
CITY OF GARDEN GROVE, CALIFORNIA
11391 ACACIA PARKWAY, P.O. BOX 3070, GARDEN GROVE, CALIFORNIA 92642

OFFICE OF THE CITY ATTORNEY
(714)741-5367

May 4, 1990

Legal Counsel
California Fair Political
Practices Commission
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Subject: Request for Advice - Financial Conflict of
Interest for Councilmember J. Tilman Williams
and Mayor Walter Donovan

Dear Legal Counsel:

The City of Garden Grove is requesting advice from the Fair Political Practices Commission about the Axiom Project and possible Financial Conflict of Interest for J. Tilman Williams and Walter Donovan.

ISSUES:

1. Does the ownership of the six parcels of land, four in the redevelopment area and two which are not, and/or his present status as an active businessman in the real estate and insurance brokerage business disqualify Councilmember J. Tilman Williams from voting on either of the following:

a. condemnation of a motel or other private property on the proposed site
b. the site plan approval
c. the development agreement
d. an amendment to the specific plan (Harbor Corridor)
e. approval of senior citizen housing and medical office building in the Axiom Development

2. Does the status of Mayor Walter Donovan as a member of the Board of Directors, a paid position for the Medical Center of Garden Grove, disqualify the Mayor from voting on either of the following:

a. condemnation of a motel or other private property on the proposed site
b. the site plan approval
c. the development agreement
d. an amendment to the specific plan (Harbor Corridor)
e. approval of senior citizen housing in the Axiom Development and medical office building

Legal Counsel
Page 2
May 4, 1990

Subject:    Request for Advice - Financial Conflict of
            Interest for Councilmember J. Tilman Williams
            and Mayor Walter Donovan

The Axiom Project itself is located at the northeast corner of
Harbor Boulevard and Palm Street (Exhibit A). Axiom Development
and Investments, Inc. proposes a mixed use development comprised
of a seven-story medical office building and a three-story 196
apartment facility for active senior citizens. The medical office
building would provide 70,000 square feet of leasable space. The
apartments will include a total of 132,000 square feet with rec-
reational facilities. The total development site consists of
6.62 acres. The proposed project adjoins the Medical Center of
Garden Grove, which is owned by American Medical International and
includes American Medical Enterprises and Palm Harbor Hospital,
Inc. on the north. The AMI facility consists of a hospital and
medical offices.

Axiom proposes to construct 70,000 square feet of medical office
space and 196 apartment units for active senior citizens. It is
our understanding that the existing medical office facility's
vacancy ratios are within accepted industry standards. However,
the construction of 70,000 square feet of additional medical
office space may affect AMI's expenses and revenues through lost
office space and increased advertisement in order to compete with
a newer medical office facility. Conversely, Palm Harbor Hospital
may benefit with the construction of the senior citizens complex.

It is the opinion of the Senior Real Property Agent for the City
of Garden Grove that the development of the Axiom Project will
affect property values of all existing development within close
proximity to said proposed development. (Exhibit B)

The Axiom Project is in the Garden Grove Community Project Re-
development Plan. Axiom Project is shown by red marking on
Exhibit C. The Redevelopment Plan area is indicated by dark shad-
ings and is a city-wide project. The Axiom Project is also
in the Harbor Corridor Specific Plan, which is shown on Exhi-
bit D. The Harbor Corridor Specific Plan is an ordinance consti-
tuting both the zoning and the general designation for most
of Harbor Boulevard and adjacent areas in the City of Garden Grove.

Adjacent to the proposed Axiom Project (Exhibit E) is the existing
medical facility, the Medical Center of Garden Grove. The Medical
Center is a wholly owned subsidiary of American Medical Interna-
tional (AMI).

Legal Counsel
Page 3
May 4, 1990

Subject:    Request for Advice - Financial Conflict of
            Interest for Councilmember J. Tilman Williams
            and Mayor Walter Donovan

The Mayor, Walter Donovan, sits as a member of the Board of Directors for the Medical Center of Garden Grove. He receives compensation of 200 a month and the bylaws are included as Exhibit F.
It is his role as a paid Board Member that causes us to request a financial conflict of interest decision.

The remainder of the request is concerning Councilmember J. Tilman Williams' financial conflict.  Mr. Williams owns six (6) parcels of land in Garden Grove and has an active real estate and insurance brokerage business also in Garden Grove. The six (6) parcels are indicated on Exhibit G.  Their addresses are: 11942 and 11952 Garden Grove Boulevard; 12291 and 12311 Harbor Boulevard; 12292 and 12312 Thackery Drive. The two (2) parcels on Garden Grove Boulevard are in the Redevelopment Area, as are the two parcels fronting on Harbor Boulevard.  The two (2) parcels on Thackery Drive are not in the Redevelopment Area.

Description and zoning of properties owned by Councilmember Williams (Exhibit G):

(a)  11952 and 11942 Garden Grove Boulevard (28,750 square feet) occupied by a non-franchise fast food restaurant, located 2,500 lineal feet west of the proposed Axiom development site.

     Harbor Corridor Specific Plan Designation:
     Zoning:           TW - Transition Zone West
     Primary Use:      Residential (R-3) density based on lot size - 28,750 would allow 13 units
     Secondary Use:    Commercial (C-1) traditional retail use permitted
     Excluded Uses:    All Office-Professional

(b)  12291 and 12311 Harbor Boulevard (6,400 square feet) occupied by Mr. Williams' insurance/real estate office. Located 2,500 lineal feet north of the proposed Axiom Development site.

     Harbor Corridor Specific Plan Designation:
     Zoning:           TN - Transition Zone North
     Primary Use:      Office (excluding medically related)

(c)  12292 and 12312 Thackery. These two lots are zoned R-1 (one house per 7,200 square feet). No commercial use permitted. These two lots are outside of Specific Plan Zoning.

Legal Counsel
Page 4
May 4, 1990

Subject:    Request for Advice - Financial Conflict of
            Interest for Councilmember J. Tilman Williams
            and Mayor Walter Donovan

In the past years, our City has requested three other FPPC opinions about Councilmember Williams' properties and business interests.
Your reference numbers are A-88-181, A-84-128, A-88-040.  I
have enclosed A-88-84-128 for your research.  It was rendered on
July 17, 1984.  (Exhibit H)

Councilmember J. Tilman Williams has a real estate and insurance
brokerage business at 12291 Harbor Boulevard.  He does have
clients within the City of Garden Grove.  He has four clients who
are within 2,500 feet of the proposed Axiom site to whom he sells
auto or homeowners' policies.  His business as a whole is divided
50 percent auto policies and 50 percent homeowners' policies.  He
has no clients on the actual site of the Axiom Project (Exhibit A).

Councilmember Williams has no future business plans within the
project area and has no business expansion hopes in the project
area.  The gross premium value of his insurance writing business
is between $400,000 and $500,000 a year.

Thank you for your anticipated assistance in these matters.

Very truly yours,

STUART SCUDDER, City Attorney

[signature]
By MARILYN WICZINSKI
Acting Deputy City Attorney

Enclosures:  Exhibits A - G and aerial map 4'5" by 3' of Axiom
             Project and properties of Councilmember Williams

6344G
05/04/90

K RECEIVED
CITY ATTORNEY'S OFFICE

City of Garden Grove

APR 2 0 1990
INTER-DEPARTMENT MEMORANDUM ^' PM

AGENCY FOR COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT

To: Stuart Scudder From: Patrick P. Importuna

Dept: City Attorney Dept: Economic Development

Subject: HARBOR CORRIDOR BACKGROUND Date: April 25, 1990
INFORMATION FOR FPPC
REGARDING AXIOM DEVELOPMENT
PROJECT


In response to your request this office has prepared a summary of the effect,
if any, that the Axiom Development Project will have on American Medical
International, American Medical Enterprises and the Palm Harbor General
Hospital properties.

Axiom Development and Investments, Inc. proposes a mixed use development
comprised of a seven story medical office building and a three story
196 apartment facility for active senior citizens. The medical office
building would provide 70,000 square feet of leasable space. The apartments
will include a total of 132,000 square feet with recreational facilities.
The total development site consists of 6.62 acres. The proposed project
adjoins the American Medical International, American Medical Enterprises and
Palm Harbor Hospital, Inc. on the North. The AMI project consists of a
hospital and medical offices.

The estimated value of the existing medical complex, as disclosed by the
1989-90 Orange County secured property roll, is $49,715,786 and is allocated
as follows:

American Medical International

Assessors Parcel No. Assessed Value

231-561-14 $ 8,051,765
231-561-22 17,511,082

Total Assessed Value $25,562,847


American Medical Enterprises

Assessors Parcel No. Assessed Value

231-561-23 $ 7,634,375
231-561-24 9,300,560

Total Assessed Value $16,934,935

Stuart Scudder
April 25 1990
Page -2-

Palm Harbor Hospitals, Inc.

Assessors Parcel No.                Assessed Value

231-561-25                          $ 7,218,004

Total Assessed Value                              $ 7,218,004

How much will the Axiom project affect AMI expenses, revenues and property
values?

Axiom proposes to construct 70,000 square feet of medical office space and
196 apartment units for active senior citizens. It is our understanding that
the existing medical office facility's vacancy ratios is within accepted
industry standards. However, the construction of 70,000 square feet of
additional medical office space may affect AMI's expenses and revenues
through lost office space and increased advertisement in order to compete
with a newer medical office facility. Conversely, Palm Harbor Hospital may
benefit with the construction of the senior citizens complex.

The development of the Axiom project will affect property values of all
existing development with close proximity to said proposed development.

Also, for inclusion in the letter to FPPC on Councilman Williams
potential conflict on the Axiom Project the following information is
submitted:

1.     The Garden Grove Hospital is owned by AMI through an investment
       partnership primarily composed of the Los Angeles County Teachers
       Retirement System and the New York State Teachers Retirement System.

2.     The Axiom Development and Investments, Inc. whose President is Abe
       Shophет, has no relationship with AMI, Garden Grove Hospital or any
       other corporations in Garden Grove. In addition, no staff members,
       physicians, board members or others associated with AMI, or Garden
       Grove Hospital    have any relationship or association with Axiom
       Development.

3.     The   proposed development by Axiom will not change the Amended
       Redevelopment plan in any way.    In fact, the proposal will further the
       goals of the Plan and assist in bringing it to fruition.

4.     Description    of   proposed development by Axiom    Development and
       Investments, Inc.:

       The development site is located at the northeast corner of Harbor
       Boulevard and Palm Street.

       The proposal envisions a mixed-uses development comprised of a seven
       story medical office building and a three story 196 apartment facility
       for active senior citizens. The medical office building would provide
       70,000 square feet of leasable space. The apartments will include a
       total of 132,000 square feet with recreational facilities. Total site
       size is 228,575 square feet (6.62 acre).

Stuart Scudder
April 25, 1990
Page -3-

The property is zoned O-P (Office-Professional) and is permissive as to
the proposed mixed use.

Estimated development costs for the proposal are as follows:

Medical Office Building - $10 million
Senior Housing                - $ 9 million

5.     Zoning of properties owned by Councilman Williams:
       (a) 11942 Garden Grove Boulevard (28,750 square feet) occupied by a
           non          franchise     fast        food        restaurant.
           Located 2900 lineal feet west of the proposed Axiom development
           site.

           Zoning: R-3 Multiple Residential and C-1 Limited Commercial

           Harbor Corridor Specific Plan Designation:

           Zoning: TW -  Transition Zone West
           Primary Use:   Residential
           Secondary Use: Commercial
           Excluded Uses: All Office-Professional

       (b) 12291 Harbor Boulevard
           (6400 square feet) occupied by an insurance/real estate office.
           Located 2550 lineal feet north of proposed Axiom Development
           site.

           Harbor Corridor Specific Plan Designation:

           Zoning: TN - Transition Zone North
           Primary Use: Office (excluding medically related)

       (c) 12292 and 12312 Thackeray.  These two lots are zoned R-1 (one
           house per 7,200 square feet).  No commercial use permitted.
           These two parcels are outside of Specific Plan Zoning and
           outside the Redevelopment Project area.

Should you require additional information, please contact Armando J. Morales,
Senior Real Property Agent at (714) 741- 5126.

PATRICK P. IMPORTUNA
Deputy City Manager

By:  Armando J. Morales
     Senior Real Property Agent

PPI:AJM:sj
